sveep_sub: Systematic Voters' Education and Electoral Participation
sveep_desc: ECI's flagship voter education programme reaching crores of Indians through awareness campaigns, cultural events, and digital outreach.
nvd_desc: On January 25 every year, India celebrates National Voters Day to encourage new voters and recognise the democratic spirit.
meri_pehli_desc: A special initiative under SVEEP focusing on empowering first-time voters aged 18-25 with information and motivation.
cvigil_desc: ECI's citizen app allows voters to report violations of the Model Code of Conduct during elections with photo and video evidence.
stat_quote: India has the world's largest democratic electorate — over 96 crore registered voters.
step1_p1: You are eligible to vote if:
step1_li2: You are 18 years of age or older (as on January 1 of the qualifying year)
step1_li3: You are an ordinary resident of the constituency where you wish to enroll
step2_p1: You must register to get your name on the electoral roll. Here's how:
step2_li1: Online: Visit the official ECI portal (voters.eci.gov.in) and fill Form 6.
step2_li2: Offline: Submit Form 6 to the Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) of your assembly constituency.
step2_li3: Keep your documents ready: Passport-size photo, Identity proof, and Address proof.
step3_p1: The Electors Photo Identity Card (EPIC) is your official voter ID.
step3_li1: Once your Form 6 is processed, your EPIC will be sent to your registered address via Speed Post.
step3_li2: You can also download a digital version (e-EPIC) from the Voter Portal.
step4_p1: Your polling booth is assigned based on your residential address.
step4_li1: You can search for your polling booth using the 'Find Booth' feature in this app or on the ECI portal.
step4_li2: You will also receive a Voter Information Slip from the Booth Level Officer (BLO) before election day.
step5_p1: What to carry to the polling booth:
step5_li2: If you don't have EPIC, you can carry any of the 11 alternative photo ID documents specified by the ECI (e.g., Aadhaar, PAN card, Driving License).
step5_li3: Mobile phones and cameras are NOT allowed inside the voting compartment.
step6_li1: The Polling Officer will verify your identity.
step6_li2: Your finger will be marked with indelible ink.
step6_li3: Proceed to the EVM compartment. Press the blue button next to your candidate's name or symbol.
step6_li4: Check the VVPAT slip for 7 seconds to verify your vote was cast correctly.
tl1_desc: The ECI announces election dates, and the Model Code of Conduct comes into effect immediately.
tl2_desc: Candidates submit their nomination papers to the Returning Officer.
tl3_desc: The Returning Officer examines the papers to ensure they meet all legal requirements.
tl4_desc: Candidates are given a few days to withdraw their names if they wish to opt out.
tl5_desc: Political parties and candidates campaign to win voter support. Campaigning ends 48 hours before polling.
tl6_desc: Voters cast their votes using Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) at designated polling stations.
tl7_desc: EVMs are opened in the presence of candidates/agents, and votes are counted under strict supervision.
tl8_desc: The Returning Officer officially declares the winning candidates, concluding the election process.

profile_about_desc: One Vote is a non-partisan Election Process Education app dedicated to empowering every Indian voter with the knowledge to participate confidently in democracy. All content is based on official ECI guidelines.

chk_election_day: Carry valid ID on election day
allow_location: Allow location access to see nearby booths

chk_desc_time: Check your constituency's polling date (7 AM – 6 PM)
chk_desc_ink: Your left index finger will be marked — this prevents double voting
chk_desc_vvpat: The paper slip visible for 7 seconds confirms your vote
chk_desc_nota: You can press 'None Of The Above' if you reject all candidates
ready_to_vote_desc: You have completed all pre-vote steps. Go make your voice heard — your vote matters!
complete_before: Complete all steps before election day
ep_sub: A step-by-step guide to the democratic process
nv_sub: Everything you need to know to cast your first vote
lh_sub: Master the election process and know your rights
nv_step1_content:
You are eligible to vote if:
• You are an Indian citizen
• You are 18 years of age or older (as on January 1 of the qualifying year)
• You are ordinarily resident of the constituency where you want to register
• Your name is in the Electoral Roll of that constituency

You are NOT eligible if:
• You are a foreign national
• You have been declared of unsound mind by a court
• You have been disqualified under any law
nv_step1_hl: Age 18+ as on 1st January of the qualifying year
nv_step2_content:
Register at voters.eci.gov.in — it's FREE!

Online registration (Form 6):
1. Visit voters.eci.gov.in
2. Click 'Register as New Voter'
3. Fill Form 6 with your details
4. Upload: Proof of Age + Proof of Address
5. Submit and note your Application ID

Documents needed:
• Age proof: Aadhaar, Birth certificate, Class 10 certificate
• Address proof: Aadhaar, Ration card, Utility bill

Your Voter ID (EPIC card) will be delivered to your address after verification.
nv_step2_hl: Registration is FREE — Never pay anyone for voter registration
nv_step3_content:
The Elector's Photo Identity Card (EPIC) is your official voter ID.

What it contains:
• Your photo
• EPIC number (unique identifier)
• Your name, father's / husband's name
• Your registered address
• Your part number and serial number

Lost your EPIC?
Download e-EPIC (digital copy) from voters.eci.gov.in for free!

Voting without EPIC?
You can also vote using: Aadhaar Card, Passport, Driving Licence, PAN Card, MNREGS Job Card, or any government-issued photo ID.
nv_step3_hl: You can vote with 12 types of valid photo ID — not just Voter ID!
nv_step4_content:
Every voter is assigned a specific polling booth. You MUST vote at your assigned booth.

How to find your booth:
1. Visit voters.eci.gov.in
2. Search for 'Find your Polling Station'
3. Enter your EPIC number or name
4. Your booth address will be shown

The Voter Slip:
Before election day, your local BLO (Booth Level Officer) will deliver a Voter Information Slip to your home with:
• Your booth address
• Voting date and time
• Your serial number on the electoral roll

Use our 'Find Booth' section to locate it on the map!
nv_step4_hl: Polling booths cannot be changed — you must vote at your designated booth
nv_step5_content:
EVM = Electronic Voting Machine — India uses these since 2004.

How to vote:
1. Show your ID to the Presiding Officer
2. Your finger will be marked with indelible ink
3. You receive a ballot slip
4. Go to the EVM — candidates are listed with their party symbols
5. Press the BLUE BUTTON next to your chosen candidate
6. A BEEP confirms your vote

VVPAT:
After pressing, a paper slip with your chosen party's name and symbol appears in the VVPAT window for 7 seconds — this is your vote confirmation.

NOTA:
'None Of The Above' is the last option on every EVM. Press it if you want to reject all candidates.
nv_step5_hl: Once you press the button, your vote is final and cannot be changed
nv_step6_content:
What to carry on Election Day:
- Any valid photo ID (Voter ID, Aadhaar, Passport, etc.)
- Voter Information Slip (if you received one)
- Dress comfortably — it can take time

What NOT to do:
- Do not carry mobile phones inside the polling booth
- Do not take photos inside the booth
- Do not accept money or gifts from candidates (it is illegal)
- Do not bring campaign material to the booth

Your rights at the booth:
• You can ask the Presiding Officer to explain the process
• You can challenge any person impersonating you
• You have a right to a secret ballot — no one can ask whom you voted for
nv_step6_hl: Voting hours are typically 7 AM to 6 PM. Go early to avoid queues!
edu_evm_desc: Electronic Voting Machines — how they work and why they're secure.
edu_vvpat_desc: Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail — your vote receipt explained.
edu_nota_desc: None Of The Above — your right to reject all candidates.
edu_mcc_desc: Rules all parties and candidates must follow during elections.
edu_eci_desc: The Election Commission of India — guardian of free and fair elections.
edu_const_desc: How India is divided into Lok Sabha and Vidhan Sabha seats.
ast_greeting: Namaste! I am your One Vote Assistant. How can I help you today? You can say 'Register to vote', 'Find my booth', or 'Election process'.
ast_register_info: To register to vote, you need to fill Form 6 on the Election Commission website. It is completely free. Should I open the registration page for you?
ast_booth_info: To find your polling booth, you will need your Voter ID number. Shall I take you to the Find Booth page?
ast_process_info: The election process has 11 steps. You can read all about it in our Learn section. Shall I take you there?
ast_confirm_no: No problem. Is there anything else I can help you with?
ast_goodbye: Thank you for using One Vote. Have a great day! Jai Hind!
ast_not_understood: I'm sorry, I didn't quite catch that. You can tap one of the buttons below to choose an option.
